title: Seurat and SpatialExperiment interaction
text: vignette, demonstrate SpotGraphs can interact Seurat SpatialExperiment objects. typical workflow involve following steps: Extract x,y coordinates Seurat SpatialExperiment object. Create igraph object SpotGraph(). Perform analysis. Store results back original Seurat SpatialExperiment object.

title: Seurat and SpatialExperiment interaction
text: use example Seurat object provided package demonstrate typical workflow. First, extract x,y coordinates Create igraph object SpotGraph() Perform analysis. case, calculate number edges per spot. Finally, store results back metadata original Seurat object
code: data("scc_s1", package = "SpotGraphs") class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) ig = SpotGraph(coord[,c('x','y')]) n_edges = igraph::degree(ig) scc_s1 = AddMetaData(scc_s1, metadata = n_edges, col.name = 'degree')

title: Seurat and SpatialExperiment interaction
text: demonstrate interaction SpatialExperiment object, load object ExperimentHub. SpatialExperiment object, can follow workflow also calculate multiple statistics several analyses igraph object storing original Seurat/SpatialExperiment object. store vertex/spot-level results back igraph object V(), can extract results igraph::vertex_attr(), coerce data.frame. resulting data.frame stored back SpatialExperiment object.
code: eh = ExperimentHub::ExperimentHub() q = AnnotationHub::query(eh, 'TENxVisium') id = q$ah_id[q$title=='HumanGlioblastoma_v3.13'] spe = eh[[id]] class(spe) #> [1] "SpatialExperiment" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SpatialExperiment" coord = as.data.frame(spatialCoords(spe)) ig = SpotGraph(coord) n_edges = igraph::degree(ig) colData(spe)$degree = n_edges V(ig)$cluster = factor(cluster_louvain(ig)$membership) V(ig)$ecc = eccentricity(ig) V(ig)$centrality = centr_eigen(ig)$vector  df = as.data.frame(vertex_attr(ig)) head(df) #>                 name coord_x coord_y is_boundary cluster ecc   centrality #> 1 AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1    7438    9389       FALSE       1  69 3.244117e-01 #> 2 AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1    1802    5327        TRUE       2   6 1.817706e-16 #> 3 AAACACCAATAACTGC-1    8514    3670        TRUE       3  75 3.573227e-02 #> 4 AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1    3122    8840       FALSE       4  64 8.092829e-02 #> 5 AAACAGTGTTCCTGGG-1   10193    5323       FALSE       5  74 1.289729e-01 #> 6 AAACATTTCCCGGATT-1    8756    9044       FALSE       6  72 3.142856e-01 colData(spe) = cbind(colData(spe), df) head(colData(spe)) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 12 columns #>                                 sample_id in_tissue array_row array_col #>                               <character> <logical> <integer> <integer> #> AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE        50       102 #> AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE         3        43 #> AAACACCAATAACTGC-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE        59        19 #> AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE        14        94 #> AAACAGTGTTCCTGGG-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE        73        43 #> AAACATTTCCCGGATT-1 HumanGlioblastoma_v3..      TRUE        61        97 #>                       degree               name   coord_x   coord_y is_boundary #>                    <numeric>        <character> <integer> <integer>   <logical> #> AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1         6 AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1      7438      9389       FALSE #> AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1         2 AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1      1802      5327        TRUE #> AAACACCAATAACTGC-1         4 AAACACCAATAACTGC-1      8514      3670        TRUE #> AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1         6 AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1      3122      8840       FALSE #> AAACAGTGTTCCTGGG-1         6 AAACAGTGTTCCTGGG-1     10193      5323       FALSE #> AAACATTTCCCGGATT-1         6 AAACATTTCCCGGATT-1      8756      9044       FALSE #>                     cluster       ecc  centrality #>                    <factor> <numeric>   <numeric> #> AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1        1        69 3.24412e-01 #> AAACAATCTACTAGCA-1        2         6 1.81771e-16 #> AAACACCAATAACTGC-1        3        75 3.57323e-02 #> AAACAGAGCGACTCCT-1        4        64 8.09283e-02 #> AAACAGTGTTCCTGGG-1        5        74 1.28973e-01 #> AAACATTTCCCGGATT-1        6        72 3.14286e-01

title: Identify centers or boundaries
text: common task spatial analysis perform analysis region interest, tumor. vignette, demonstrate use NeighborhoodCenters() function help us identify characteristics center region interest boundary.

title: Identify centers or boundaries
text: use 10X Visium dataset, downloaded GSE208253, demonstrate NeighborhoodCenters().
code: data("scc_s1", package = "SpotGraphs") scc_s1 = UpdateSeuratObject(scc_s1) #> Warning: Not validating Centroids objects class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" dim(scc_s1) #> [1] 36601  1185  coord = Seurat::GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = data.frame(x = coord$x, y = -coord$y) head(coord) #>       x      y #> 1  4809 -16571 #> 2  3944 -13546 #> 3  8142 -14812 #> 4 13505 -10536 #> 5 11764 -13053 #> 6  4240  -9011

title: Identify centers or boundaries
text: vignette, use clustering select region interest tissue sample, practice, instead region biological interest. region interest, .e., neighborhood, provided NeighborhoodCenters() named vector TRUE/FALSE values, names correspond spot barcodes given dataset.
code: # create an igraph object with SpotGraph() and perform clustering ig = SpotGraph(coord) cl = igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig) cl = factor(cl$membership)  # select cluster 1 and name the boolean vector with vertex names roi = cl==5 names(roi) = names(igraph::V(ig))

title: Identify centers or boundaries
text: output function returns list, first element data.frame scores igraph::centr_eigen() is_neighborhood == T, 0 is_neighborhood == F.
code: res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = coord, is_neighborhood = roi) print(head(res$eigen.scores)) #>   barcode center_eigen #> 1       1            0 #> 2       2            0 #> 3       3            0 #> 4       4            0 #> 5       5            0 #> 6       6            0  # Set centrality scores as a vertex attribute in our igraph object igraph::V(ig)$centr_eigen = res$eigen.scores$center_eigen igraph::V(ig)$roi_center = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$centers igraph::V(ig)$roi_boundary = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$boundary  plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'centr_eigen',                         pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_viridis() +   theme_void() plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_center',                         pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none') plt3 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_boundary',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void()  wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3, design = 'A#\nBC')

title: Identify centers or boundaries
text: NeighborhoodCenters() function can also identify characteristics multiple regions providing named boolean vector indicating whether spot within regions. can useful several tumor regions within tissue section. , select three different clusters regions interest, instead just one.
code: roi = cl %in% c('1', '2', '5') names(roi) = names(igraph::V(ig)) res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = coord, is_neighborhood = roi) # Set centrality scores as a vertex attribute in our igraph object igraph::V(ig)$centr_eigen = res$eigen.scores$center_eigen igraph::V(ig)$roi_center = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$centers igraph::V(ig)$roi_boundary = names(igraph::V(ig)) %in% res$boundary  plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'centr_eigen',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_viridis() +   theme_void() plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_center',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = 'none') plt3 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_boundary',                          pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c('grey', 'red')) +   theme_void()  wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3, design = 'A#\nBC')

what: 5. Calculate distance between each spot and ROI center
title: Identify centers or boundaries
code: roi_dist = igraph::distances(ig, weights = NA)[,res$centers] %>%    apply(1, min) boundary_dist = igraph::distances(ig, weights = NA)[,res$boundary] %>%    apply(1, min) # boundary_dist = ifelse(!roi, boundary_dist, 0)  igraph::V(ig)$roi_dist = roi_dist igraph::V(ig)$boundary_dist = boundary_dist  plt.dist = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'roi_dist',                  pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_distiller(palette = 'RdBu', direction = 1) +   ggtitle('distance to ROI centers') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none') plt.boundarydist = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'boundary_dist',                  pt.size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_color_distiller(palette = 'RdBu', direction = 1) +   ggtitle('distance to ROI boundaries') +   guides(color = guide_colorbar(title = 'distance'),          alpha = guide_none()) +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'))  wrap_plots(plt.dist, plt.boundarydist, nrow = 1)

title: SpotGraphs
text: demonstrate general usage SpotGraph, use 10X Visium dataset squamous cell carcinoma patient, downloaded GSE208253. raw data GEO provided Seurat object package use example.
code: data("scc_s1", package = "SpotGraphs") scc_s1 = UpdateSeuratObject(scc_s1) #> Warning: Not validating Centroids objects class(scc_s1) #> [1] "Seurat" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "SeuratObject" dim(scc_s1) #> [1] 36601  1185

title: SpotGraphs
text: goal package support Visium analysis providing function constructs igraph object based number immediately adjacent spots slide. opens analysis pipeline various igraph functions perform certain tasks : identifying spots lying tissue debris removed downstream analysis distance calculations spots measuring shortest path network spot easily identifying edges pre-defined regions tissue construct igraph object, need x,y coordinates spots slide. can plot igraph object ggnetwork package, interfaces directly ggplot2, use SpatialPlotGraph function provided package visualize edges drawn node original x,y coordinates stored igraph object. Note is_boundary attribute automatically calculated running SpotGraph(), use color node plots.

title: SpotGraphs
text: One main features SpotGraph package identify spots slide lie top tissue debris disconnected rest tissue sample. uninformative technical artifacts sample processing, ’d like exclude downstream analysis. can identify whether spot surrounded spots using igraph::degree() function, filter spots adjacent one spot igraph::subgraph(): First, extract x,y coordinates spot dataset Create igraph object SpotGraph() Calculate number connections per-spot igraph::degree() can now plot number connections (.e., results igraph::degree()) storing results back original Seurat object started . Additionally, can add another metadata column indicate whether spot adjacent one spot. can now observe results igraph::degree() Seurat::SpatialFeaturePlot(). Spots colored red spots ’ve identified one adjacent spot slide. method, able remove low quality spots data, entirely based spot-level adjacencies.  Alternatively, perform clustering using spot adjacencies identified SpotGraph(). set resolution = 0 group spots together connected way. can store clustering results back Seurat object , plot Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(). clustering, can choose keep clusters 1 2, let us remove low quality spots disconnected tissue sample data.  apply filter determined using igraph clustering method.
code: coord = GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) ig = SpotGraph(coord[,1:2])  n_connections = igraph::degree(ig) scc_s1 = AddMetaData(scc_s1, n_connections, 'degree') scc_s1 = AddMetaData(scc_s1, n_connections>1, 'adj_threshold') plt.deg = SpatialFeaturePlot(scc_s1, feature = 'degree', image.alpha = 0.6) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = 'Blues') plt.degfilter = SpatialDimPlot(scc_s1, group.by = 'adj_threshold', image.alpha = 0.6) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c('red', 'grey90')) wrap_plots(plt.deg, plt.degfilter) cl = igraph::cluster_louvain(ig, resolution = 0) scc_s1 = AddMetaData(scc_s1, factor(cl$membership), 'igraph_clusters') plt.cl = SpatialDimPlot(   object = scc_s1,    group.by = 'igraph_clusters',    image.alpha = 0.6,    label = T,    label.size = 3 ) + NoLegend()     scc_s1 = AddMetaData(scc_s1, cl$membership %in% c('1','2'), 'cl_threshold') plt.clfitler = SpatialDimPlot(   object = scc_s1,    group.by = 'cl_threshold',    image.alpha = 0.6 ) + scale_fill_manual(values = c('red', 'grey90'))  wrap_plots(plt.cl, plt.clfitler) # Apply filter to keep igraph clusters 1 and 2 scc_s1 = subset(scc_s1, subset = cl_threshold)

title: SpotGraphs
text: working igraph object, may desirable remove edges two groups spots.
code: # Create igraph object coord = GetTissueCoordinates(scc_s1) coord = data.frame(x = coord$x, y = -coord$y) ig = SpotGraph(coord)  # Clustering with igraph igraph::V(ig)$clusters = factor(igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig)$membership) plt1 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('before') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  # Remove edges between pairs of clusters ig = CutEdges(ig, cluster_pairs = list(c(4,7), c(4,3), c(4,8)), cluster.col = 'clusters') plt2 = SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'clusters', label = T) +   ggtitle('after') +   theme_void() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = 'bold'),         legend.position = 'none')  plt3 = plt2 +    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.15,0.65), ylim = c(0.15, 0.65)) +   ggtitle('after/zoomed') wrap_plots(plt1, plt2, plt3)

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: Current spatial transcriptomic analysis pipelines R focus pre-processing visualization, providing limited tools interact “spatial” aspect data. address limitation, provide set tools allow flexibility working spatial coordinates enable filtering low quality spots tissue debris, edit spot-level adjacencies, identify centers boundaries user-defined neighborhoods interest. package allows users simply provide x,y-coordinates spatial data create igraph object SpotGraph() function, various graph-based statistics can calculated stored meta data user’s original Seurat SpatialExperiment object.

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: construct graph given x,y-coordinates spatial data, SpotGraph() function implements two approaches identifying neighboring spots build adjacency matrix, either based Euclidean distance Delaunay triangulation. See manuscript details.

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: install latest version package, run: devtools::install_github("Sanin-Lab/SpotGraphs")

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: create igraph object spatial data, required input SpotGraph() function data frame matrix two columns corresponding x,y-coordinates. ig = SpotGraph(coord.df) igraph object, can now apply igraph functions, can either stored igraph object, stored additional meta data columns. example, one calculate number connections (.e., adjacent spots) per spot: V(ig)$degree = igraph::degree(ig) perform clustering, given spot-level adjacencies: cl = igraph::cluster_fast_greedy(ig) can stored back original coordinates data frame: coord.df$igraph_clusters = factor(cl$membership)

title: Generate igraph Objects With Visium Spot Coordinates For Basic Analysis Tasks
text: See documentation website -depth walkthroughs.

what: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: Remove edges lie pairs clusters

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
code: CutEdges(igraph_object, cluster_pairs = NULL, cluster.col = "cluster")

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: igraph_object igraph object cluster results stored. cluster_pairs list containing two-element vectors indicating pairs cluster names want remove edges . cluster.col character value vertex attribute name igraph object clustering results.

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
text: updated igraph object edges removed

title: Remove all edges that lie between pairs of clusters — CutEdges
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Create an igraph object with these coordinates ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Perform clustering and add those cluster assignments to the igraph object cl = igraph::cluster_louvain(ig)$membership ig = igraph::set_vertex_attr(ig, 'cluster', value = factor(cl))  # Examine graph before removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster')  # Remove edges between clusters 1 and 2, and between clusters 3 and 4 ig = CutEdges(igraph_object = ig,               cluster_pairs = list(c(1,2), c(1,3)),               cluster.col = 'cluster')  # Examine graph after removing edges SpatialPlotGraph(ig, group.by = 'cluster') } # }

what: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
text: Score spots using igraph::centr_eigen identify spots center region/neighborhood.

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
code: NeighborhoodCenters(coord = NULL, is_neighborhood)

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates, rownames spot barcodes/ids. is_neighborhood boolean vector (TRUE/FALSE) equal length number rows coord. Centers identified neighborhood spots labeled TRUE.

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
text: list following elements: eigen.scores: numeric vector output igraph::centr_eigen, score spot, max(eigen.scores) within neighborhood identify center. centers: character vector barcode ids spots identified center neighborhood spots. boundaries: character vector barcode ids spots identified boundary neighborhood spots.

title: Identify center of some neighborhood of spots, e.g., the center of a tumor — NeighborhoodCenters
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y') nspots = dim(df)[1] rownames(df) = paste0('spot', 1:nspots)  igraph::V(ig)$cluster = igraph::cluster_leiden(ig, resolution = 0)$membership is_cluster = igraph::V(ig)$cluster=='1' names(is_cluster) = names(igraph::V(ig))  res = NeighborhoodCenters(coord = df, is_neighborhood = is_cluster) } # }

title: Visualize igraph vertex attribute on tissue x,y coordinates — SpatialPlotGraph
text: igraph_object igraph object coord (optional) two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. provided, look coord_x coord_y attributes provided igraph object. group.vertex attribute plot onto tissue coordinates. Must present vertex_attr(ig). label Logical, whether label groups plot based group.. linewidth Numeric input determine width segments nodes. Used geom_segment(). pt.size Numeric input determine size point plot. Used size parameter geom_point().

title: Visualize igraph vertex attribute on tissue x,y coordinates — SpatialPlotGraph
text: ggplot object

title: Visualize igraph vertex attribute on tissue x,y coordinates — SpatialPlotGraph
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame and create and igraph object df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y') ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Plot cluster results SpatialPlotGraph(igraph_object = ig, group.by = 'is_boundary') } # }

what: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: Create igraph object x,y coordinates, assuming adjacent spots equidistant , drawing edges spots immediately adjacent .

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
code: SpotGraph(   coord,   delaunay = F,   delaunay.trim = T,   dist.buffer = 1.05,   max.dist = NULL )

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: coord two-column data.frame matrix, column contains x y coordinates. delaunay Whether use Delaunay triangulation identify edges network construction. Default FALSE. delaunay.trim TRUE (default) delaunay = TRUE, edge lengths longer max.dist removed Delaunay triangulation. dist.buffer Influences maximum distance node allowed another node considered neighbor. used max.dist = NULL. max.dist furthest node another node considered neighbors. NULL (default), shortest distance two nodes minimum distance (min.dist) required two nodes considered neighbors, max.dist calculated hypotenuse triangle two sides length min.dist, multiplied dist.buffer.

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
text: igraph object, vertex (.e., node) corresponds row coord input, un-weighted edges vertices immediately adjacent .

title: Creates an igraph object given x,y coordinates — SpotGraph
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a coordinate data frame with two isolated groups of points df = rbind(expand.grid(1:5, 1:5), expand.grid(9:11, 9:11)) colnames(df) = c('x', 'y')  # Preview the points on a grid with their corresponding x,y coordinates ggplot(df, aes(x = x,y=y)) +   geom_point()  # Identify points that are immediately adjacent to each other and create an igraph object ig = SpotGraph(df)  # Optionally view the network with ggnetwork # library(ggnetwork) ggplot(ig, aes(x=x, y=y, xend=xend, yend=yend)) +   geom_edges() +   geom_nodes() } # }

what: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
text: Calculate threshold given set statistics

title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
code: get_threshold(   stats,   percentile1 = 0.05,   percentile2 = 0.95,   log = T,   print.plot = T )

title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
text: stats numeric vector. percentile1 percentile set lower bound calculating local minimum. percentile2 percentile set upper bound calculating local minimum. log logical (default TRUE), whether use log input statistics calculating threshold; useful calculating threshold filtering spots based number transcripts detected per-spot. print.plot logical (default TRUE), whether print plot approximated function intervals (red) threshold (blue) plotted.

title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
text: local minimum approximated function percentiles set percentile1 percentile2. plot approximated function also printed print.plot == TRUE returned value intervals plotted.

title: Calculate a threshold given some set of statistics — get_threshold
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # To set a threshold for number of transcripts per spot to filter low quality spots: seu_obj = scc_s1 nCount = seu_obj$nCount_Spatial thres = get_threshold(nCount) seu_obj_filtered = subset(seu_obj, nCount_Spatial > thres) } # }

text: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE208253
